conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have a child, Alex, who's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To continue assessing your eligibility, I need to ask:

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great. Now I need to understand your living arrangement with Alex:

Does Alex live with you, or does Alex live with someone else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex doesn't live with me - he lives with his ex-partner.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. This is an important factor in determining eligibility.

Are you contributing towards Alex's upbringing in any way, such as paying at least the equivalent of Child Benefit in maintenance or supporting Alex financially?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do help out a bit with some money for things Alex needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To better assess your situation, I need to understand a bit more:

Are you paying at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount regularly? (Child Benefit is currently £25.60 per week for the eldest/only child, or about £111 per month). And does your ex-partner currently claim Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I pay £10 a week towards Alex's needs. And yes, I believe my ex-partner is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

- While Alex is 8 years old and meets the age requirement (under 16), he doesn't live with you - he lives with your ex-partner
- Your ex-partner is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- **Only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child**, and priority is given to the person the child lives with
- Although you do contribute financially (£10 per week), this doesn't change the eligibility rules, and it's also below the current Child Benefit rate of £25.60 per week

In situations where a child lives with someone else who is already claiming Child Benefit, the parent who doesn't live with the child cannot also claim it, even if they contribute financially to the child's upbringing.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05); Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which meets the age requirement (under 16). However, Alex does not live with you - Alex lives with your ex-partner. According to the rules, only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child. Your ex-partner is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex. While you do contribute financially (£10 per week), this is less than the Child Benefit amount (£25.60 per week for an eldest/only child), and more importantly, when a child lives with someone else who is already claiming Child Benefit, you cannot also claim it. Only one person can get Child Benefit for the same child, and priority is typically given to the person the child lives with. Therefore, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: You are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. While Alex meets the age criteria and you are contributing financially, Alex lives with your ex-partner who is already claiming Child Benefit. Only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child, and the person the child lives with has priority. Your contribution of £10 per week, while helpful, is also below the Child Benefit rate and does not change the eligibility rules in this situation.
